content:
{
"thoughts": "I have just listed the project files and identified the key manifests (README.md, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, setup.py, etc.). The next step is to create a Dockerfile that will build an image, clone the repository (already present locally but we will clone again inside the container for reproducibility), install system packages (git, python3, pip, venv, build-essential, graphviz), set up a virtual environment, install Python dependencies, and be ready to run the test suite. I will now write the Dockerfile using the provided template, inserting the repository URL and project directory name.",
  "command": {
    "name": "write_to_file",
    "args": {
      "filename": "Dockerfile",
      "text": "FROM ubuntu:24.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    python3 python3-pip python3-venv build-essential graphviz \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

ARG REPO_URL
ARG PROJECT_DIR=Scalpel

RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}

CMD ["/bin/bash"]"
    }
  }
}

{
"thoughts": "The previous Docker build failed because the build argument REPO_URL was not provided, causing the git clone step to abort. To fix this, I will rewrite the Dockerfile so that REPO_URL has a sensible default value (the actual repository URL for Scalpel) while still allowing it to be overridden via a build argument. This ensures the git clone command can succeed when the image is built. After updating the Dockerfile, I will write it to the file system so that the subsequent build can proceed without errors.",
  "command": {
    "name": "write_to_file",
    "args": {
      "filename": "Dockerfile",
      "text": "FROM ubuntu:24.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    tzdata bash ca-certificates \
    python3 python3-pip python3-venv build-essential graphviz \
    && ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/SMAT-Lab/Scalpel.git
ARG PROJECT_DIR=Scalpel

RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}

CMD ["/bin/bash"]"
    }
  }
}
